data.content:
The fastest workflow is **`dotnet watch test`** — it monitors your files and re-runs tests automatically on every save, no manual rebuild needed.

Let me start it so you can see the failing test, then apply the fix:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-619add6993294359b782ba9db9968b55 && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-test"}

data.content: Good — `dotnet watch` is running and waiting for changes. There are two issues: a missing `using System;` and the actual bug (no negative amount validation). Let me fix both:
data.reasoningText: There's a missing `using System;` directive. Let me fix that first, then fix the actual bug. Since `dotnet watch` is running, it will automatically re-run after I save the file.

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
